tweet: We must break out of the paradigm of the military–industrial–congressional complex. Without such reform, we will permit those innovative warriors of other nations such as China to command future warfare.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We need smart defense reform that can allow us to do more with less – focusing on cyberspace, sensors, robotics, artificial intelligence and other new technologies that reflect what our military needs today.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: In fact, our U.S. Commander of the Pacific has said America no longer commands the Western Pacific, China does — the first loss of command of the seas by the U.S. Navy since World War II. And he has said that we need more cyberspace and better positioning of our force posture.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: An investment in the new domain of cyberspace from just 10 carrier equivalents (1 carrier “unit”) would provide an immense growth in warfare capability.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Take the most powerful Navy “unit" — the aircraft carrier. Today, one carrier can strike eight times the targets in 24 hours as it could two decades ago — giving us 80 carrier equivalents as compared to just 10 carriers a few years ago.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Precisely how such technological capability affects traditional warfare is why numbers as the measure of our military might are obsolete.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Some of the greatest national security threats we face cannot be defeated or defended by traditional military hardware, but only by greatly enhanced cyberspace warfare, including both offensive cyber-warfare and cyber-security.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We need to realize that the key battlefield today is no longer necessarily on land, on air or on sea – it is in cyberspace.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: This is even more germane as water becomes translucent, eventually transparent, by emerging technologies. Rather than double-down on less relevant, expensive units, we must invest in new technology that gives us better capability, not just more unnecessary capacity at more cost.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: For example, rather than buying more submarines at $2 billion each, we can develop a netted sensor information system to track enemy submarines, and then direct an aircraft to drop a torpedo for the “kill.”

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Instead of measuring military readiness in terms of overall size and numbers, we must focus on metrics of capability, particularly in technology, and positioning.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The issue is not how much money we are spending, but how we are spending it. We need to transform our military forces by focusing on force posture, not force structure.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Our military still ranks as the best in the world, but it is losing that position because too many vested interests hold it back from responding to the challenges of tomorrow instead of those of yesteryear.

tweet: Other than during the Civil War, it's hard to imagine a time when our nation has been less unified. But one thing that always binds us together is the abiding respect we all share for our veterans.

tweet: If we are to restore the American Dream for those living between our two coasts in Middle America, and for those in the innermost of our inner cities, we must reverse America’s retreat from the world for our collective good here at home.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: By bringing together those that shared these values in multilateral organizations and agreements, and by leading those efforts, we all became stronger, safer, healthier and more prosperous in our freedoms. And that – that, is what really makes "America First."

tweet: Whether Stalinism or human rights atrocities, dire poverty or world recessions, we convened the world by the power of these ideals.

tweet: They kept their promise by building the liberal world order based upon the rules of individual and human rights, open and fair markets, fair and just governments. It was one that embraced the world’s collective good.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: That is what America had been doing ever since the world’s greatest generation had returned home from having defeated the horrors of fascism and imperialism in the second of the world wars. And they swore it wouldn’t happen, again, to us, a third time.

tweet: We need to ensure that "OSHA legal" actually means "safe", not merely “legal”.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Hundreds of workers in microwave popcorn factories have developed cancer and died after exposure to chemicals, a situation that one professional said was equivalent to inhaling acid — a tragedy that may never have happened if we had a more robust OSHA.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Over 5,000 workers died on the job in 2017, and an additional 50,000 workers who are estimated to die every year from occupational diseases caused by chemical exposure -- more than die from guns or even car accidents.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: OSHA has lacked the leadership and will to provide appropriate oversight of it for far too long: there hasn't been a major update to the OSHA law since it was first enacted in 1970.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Corporations like Amazon should not be treating time management of their products better than their workforce. Worker safety is a serious issue for the Department of Labor and OSHA (the Occupational Safety & Health Administration).

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Through public education, federal student loans, and a raft of other programs, nearly every worker in this country has reached their current position in part because of public investment in them, and we forfeit our nation's investment when we allow any worker to be left behind.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: My plan includes a range of labor training solutions: more public-private partnerships, more funding for state and local programs, more registered apprenticeships on federally-funded building or infrastructure repair projects, and a national apprenticeship program.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Automation & globalization bring job losses, so we need "Training for a Lifetime" to train & re-train workers for jobs available now. That's what we do in the military when a plane or ship becomes obsolete, with the largest community college in the country - run by the Air Force.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We currently spend just .001% of our GDP on labor training, the lowest of all developed countries. Yet there are great models around the country pointing to ways to do better (on my website I explain the story of ITWorks, a project in Philadelphia, Wilmington, & Las Vegas).

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We need a system-wide transformation of our national job training infrastructure, and that's what my plan entails. Massive public and private investment are needed to help fund more places like this fine institution here in New Hampshire.  https://t.co/bD4kP8hzhP

name: Joe Sestak

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Comprehensive immigration reform should include a path to citizenship for undocumented immigrants who pay back taxes, pass criminal background checks, prove gainful employment, and pass a basic English language test. And Dreamers deserve immediate relief with permanent status.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Without immigration, our country would be in a population death spiral like Japan. Critical industries from farming and food processing to home health care rely on immigrant labor. We need them. We should increase guest worker visas as well as visas for skilled STEM workers.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: As the son of an immigrant (now buried at Arlington), I know that the immigrant story is the story of America. Our immigration system may be broken, but it will not be fixed by a wall or mass deportations. We need bipartisan comprehensive immigration reform and we need it soon.

name: Joe Sestak

tweet: China wants to be the world's predominant superpower — and 5G is clearly a tool they intend to use to reach that goal. Countries around the world should beware.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Advanced countries like German y — and America — must incentivize domestic production of the tech necessary to build a modern 5G network and ensure that China has no access into critical infrastructure & communications.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: China’s ownership will give it a police-state capability to surveil everything on the network, both for commercial and intelligence purposes: whether it is to observe virtual business meetings or to close down critical infrastructure during international tensions.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: This decision, based on short-term profits, has devastating long-term consequences because whoever builds the 5G network will own it, able to see everything that flows through the wireless equipment that the United States does not even make!

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Unfortunately for us, Germany is making a huge national security mistake in allowing Huawei technology to be used in its 5G mobile networks, an internet system that will revolutionize economies — and warfare.  https://t.co/0TxtqWVdjG

tweet: That's what I wanted for the Navy two decades ago, and it's what we still need today — more capability at less cost.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: To deal with the threats of the 21st century -- including a rising China and other autocracies, rogue states, and violent extremists -- we must lead in high technology, especially the cyber field.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: A better military is about better capability, not just capacity or numbers. Our aircraft carriers were able to strike nearly 10 times the targets in 24 hours as a two decades earlier, and the future was where our oceans were becoming increasingly transparent.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: ... while allocating substantially more resources toward critical technologies -- from high-tech sensors to robotics and especially, cyberspace warfare.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: It was a transformation from basing our prowess upon force structure and instead force posture and capability. After analyzing and war-gaming all of the available data, I determined that we didn't need 375 but rather a reduction of the fleet to 260...

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: At times, I am asked about the military budget and our capability.  I believe that we do need Defense Reform, much as I proposed in 2005 as the three star Admiral in charge of the Navy’s $350 billion warfare requirements.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The Cheshire County, NH, facility is a credit to the pragmatic progressive ideals that made it what it is. But it is one facility. We need to rethink our entire criminal justice system, so we best address concerns of, and with, those incarcerated, while reducing recidivism rates.  https://t.co/evF0mXZWOw

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: There are fewer psychiatric beds in America today than we had in the 1850s. And 50% of psychiatrists will not admit patients unless they pay in cash.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Mental health is a major issue why so many people end up in prison. The largest psychiatric hospital in America today is Rikers Island prison in NY. We have shuttled too many people out of care or denied them it and left it to our prisons to handle.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: I visited a prison every year as a Congressman and afterwards. So many people like our vets from Vietnam ended up there due to PTSD - we didn't know what it was at the time.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Today the United States locks up 2.4 million people in our prisons. We have the highest incarceration rate in the world. Compounding the problem is the fact that about half of these prisoners will end up back in jail after they are released. This is an immense problem.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: It’s so important that people coming out of prison not only get a fair chance from employers willing to take them on, but that they are able to earn a decent wage. 99% of people want to live good lives. We have to help them.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Yet with the greatest will in the world, it’s very hard to make it with a record. People need jobs that actually provide a decent living, or they’ll too easily self-medicate or go back to a life of crime.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: It is not just for the individual to become productive — the entire nation benefits from their being given a second chance to contribute to society.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: I also support restoring the right for ex-felons to vote, because participating in the political process is such a great way to send a message that we value people’s productive role they can play in society.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The best way to avoid reoffending is to give prisoners the tools to succeed on the outside once they have done their time.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The superintendent’s approach is how best to manage behavior — not trying to control, but often deescalate. We need to make prison a truly rehabilitative experience, with more funding for mental health, drug treatment and vocational training and education.

name: Joe Sestak

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: I mourn our loss that fateful day of 9/11; and I also honor my fellow Americans who cherish our ideals of freedom — the values of individual and human rights and fair and just governments — while embracing the world’s collective good that helps us all.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: As I arrived in the Arabian Sea that day, it reminded me of how America is seen around the world: a nation that espouses the ideals that many can only yearn for and dream of.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We are the ideal of the world, and we must never forget that. 9/11 was an attack upon whom we are, and what America stands for, by those who abhor such values.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: We had the world with us — united against evil — because in that distant sea was the realization that America’s greatest power is its power to convene, to bring the peoples and nations of the world together for a common cause that serves us all.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Nations had sent ships from around the globe, some crossing the Atlantic like Canada, and others, came from the Pacific, such as Australia.  The Italian Minister of Defense said it well: “We are coming because America has been attacked, and we will be there for them.”

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Waiting there for us to become part of our American battle group was an international armada. The Japanese were there – whose ships had not been out of the Sea of Japan since the Second World War. Alongside them were Germany and Great Britain.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Then leading our sons and daughters in harm’s way as Commander of an aircraft carrier battle group. And when we arrived in the Arabian Sea to begin our strikes against Afghanistan, my 10,000 sailors, SEALS and Marines experienced what I had seen wherever I had served my country.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: It took me to Afghanistan for a very brief period as the war started, gathering with SEALs in broken down farmhouses, and assessing rules of engagement for our hi-tech airpower in this very different Global War on Terror on the ground.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: That day, the world changed for all of us. I was told to establish the Navy’s strategic anti-terrorism unit.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: I lost some I had known in those offices I had worked in the previous year, as Americans across our land lost loved ones on a day we must never forget for that loss, for the heroism by so many who fought to save others, and for what might have been for those who perished.

tweet: We’re just doing what our forefathers taught us for a more healthy and productive workforce. Health security keeps us competitive. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: It’s why in 1790 the very 1st Congress mandated health care for merchant sailors – the backbone of our new maritime nation. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Every enrollment period will bring us closer to universal coverage, increasing the pool and bending the cost curve along the way. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: (Pt 1 of 3)  Reducing # of uninsured is crucial b/c cost to our economy of underinsurance is estimated to be $65 to $130 billion annually.

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: (Pt 2 of 3)  So it's a big deal that 6 in 10 people who bought health insurance through ACA exchanges were previously uninsured. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: (Pt 3 of 3) To that end, today the uninsured rate has fallen from about 20 percent to about 14 percent. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The growth rate in premiums charged in Medicare Part B is at almost 0%. Spending rose by just $1 per beneficiary in 2013. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: B/c of penalties for high readmit rates, hospital errors are ↓ 17% since 2010—saving 50K lives & $12 billion to the health system. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Because hospitals are now incentivized to avoid readmitting patients, 150,000 fewer patients were readmitted in 2012 and 2013. #ACA4PA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Even though coverage under Medicare hospital insurance program was extended to extra 1M ppl in 2013, it spent LESS in '13 than '12. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: The ACA has added *13 years* to the life of the Medicare trust fund!  Before ACA passed: 2017  Now: 2030  #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Re last tweet, DHHS estimates consumers wld’ve spent almost $4 billion more on premiums in '13 if insurance companies kept 2011 MLR #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Medical Loss Ratio requires Ins Cos 2 spend 80% of premiums on care instd of bonuses. In '14, 7M ppl received $330M in MLR rebates #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: By closing “donut hole” in Medicare drug coverage, 8 million seniors & ppl w disabilities saved $12 billion since '10, avg of $1500 #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: In marketplaces for small biz, plans through SHOP exchanges have premiums 7% less expensive than plans sold off the marketplaces. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: ↑ insurers on exchanges = ↑ competition = ↓ prices. 44-state analysis shows # of new marketplace insurers 5X greater than # exiting #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: B4 reform, double-digit annual premium increases were the norm. Now, nat’l avg premium increase for 2015 is estimated to be 7.5%. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Exchange premiums are estimated to be 15% lower in '16 than CBO expected in '10. That's $100Bn extra budget savings over nxt decade #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: For 2015, more than 7 in 10 marketplace enrollees had lower-premium plans available at the same category of 2014 coverage. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Average premiums in the health exchanges are 4% lower than average premiums for similar employer-sponsored coverage. #ACA4USA

name: Joe Sestak
tweet: Since ACA passed, we’ve cut in half per capita health care cost growth rate of prior 8 yrs: from 6% to 3%. Slowest rise in 50 yrs! #ACA4USA
